findings.summary: agents/cli/handlers.py has 15 public wrapper functions that each forward identically into 3 private helpers, creating a high-blast-radius pass-through hub with no added value at the seam.
findings.evidence: 15 public functions (handle_jules_execute, handle_claude_execute, etc.) all delegate to _handle_agent_execute with (client_class, client_name, args) — same parameter bag, no transformation. This means any change to _handle_agent_execute signature breaks all 15 callers simultaneously.
findings.suggestion: Replace the 15 public wrappers with a single dispatch function that accepts an agent type enum, or expose _handle_agent_execute directly and let the 3 concrete handlers be the public surface.

findings.summary: 3 real import cycles detected: dependency_injection/container↔scopes, utils/process/subprocess↔subprocess_advanced, api/openapi_generator↔openapi_standardization_generator.
findings.evidence: desloppify scan reports 18 import cycle instances (3 unique real cycles + worktree duplicates). Import cycles force both modules to be partially loaded at import time, creating fragile initialization order dependencies.
findings.suggestion: Break each cycle by extracting shared types/constants into a third neutral module (e.g., dependency_injection/types.py), or use TYPE_CHECKING guards for forward references.

findings.summary: camelCase identifier leaks in transport (9 instances) and lora (5 instances) modules break the otherwise consistent snake_case convention across the codebase.
findings.evidence: Codebase is overwhelmingly snake_case. Isolated camelCase leaks: transport/9, lora/5, performance/5, git_operations/1, model_context_protocol/1, terminal_interface/1. cerebrum/core/utils.py places module docstring after the logger line rather than at the top of the file.
findings.suggestion: Rename camelCase identifiers in transport/ and lora/ to snake_case. Move the module docstring in cerebrum/core/utils.py to the very top of the file (before any imports).

findings.summary: bridge/ directory has 13 returns_null and 10 try_catch patterns with 0 throws — errors are silently swallowed rather than propagated, violating the project's zero-silent-fallback policy.
findings.evidence: bridge/ has 13 returns_null, 10 try_catch, 0 throws. cloud/infomaniak/ subpackages use returns_null almost exclusively. cli/utils.py defines ImportError fallback stubs for TerminalFormatter/CommandRunner that return None silently — directly contradicting CLAUDE.md zero-silent-fallbacks policy.
findings.suggestion: Audit bridge/ for except blocks that return None or empty dict instead of re-raising. In cli/utils.py, replace the ImportError stub classes with explicit `raise ImportError(...)` so callers know the dependency is missing.

findings.summary: The same module directories mix try_catch, throws, returns_null, and result_type with no documented policy, making error behavior unpredictable across the service layer.
findings.evidence: cloud/infomaniak/ compute/dns/network/object_storage all use returns_null almost exclusively with no throws. Other modules in the same layer (orchestrator, agents) use raises. No per-layer or per-module error strategy is documented.
findings.suggestion: Add an error strategy declaration to each module's AGENTS.md (e.g., 'This module raises on all errors'). Pick raises as the default for service-layer modules — returns_null is appropriate only for pure query functions.

findings.summary: advanced_plotter.py has 9 wide functions with 452 config_bag instances total. PlotConfig dataclass exists to address this but is bypassed in the wide methods themselves.
findings.evidence: 1,918 total indirection hotspots in the codebase. advanced_plotter.py has 9 wide functions with 50 config_bag mentions. PlotConfig was added as a dataclass solution but create_advanced_chart and related methods still accept individual keyword arguments rather than a config object.
findings.suggestion: Refactor advanced_plotter.py wide functions to accept PlotConfig as the first argument. Provide a builder or dataclass_fields helper for callers who prefer keyword style.

findings.summary: events/handlers/event_logger.py wraps get_event_stats and get_recent_events as single-line delegators with no transformation — pure indirection with no abstraction value.
findings.evidence: get_event_stats() and get_recent_events() in EventLogger each consist of a single return self.event_bus.get_*() call with no additional logic, type conversion, or filtering. These wrappers add a layer of indirection without any benefit.
findings.suggestion: Remove the delegator wrappers and let callers access self.event_bus directly, or add genuine value (filtering, projection, error handling) to justify the indirection.

findings.summary: advanced_plotter.py hard-imports matplotlib, numpy, pandas, and seaborn at module level, pulling the full ML/viz stack into any module that imports it.
findings.evidence: Four heavy dependencies (matplotlib, numpy, pandas, seaborn) are unconditionally imported at the top of advanced_plotter.py. Any module that does `from data_visualization import X` will trigger all four imports even if it only needs a non-plotting utility.
findings.suggestion: Move matplotlib/numpy/pandas/seaborn imports inside the functions that use them, or split advanced_plotter.py into a lightweight entry module and a heavy rendering submodule that's only imported when needed.

findings.summary: openapi_generator.py assigns None to RESTAPI and GraphQLAPI on ImportError, creating a silent degradation that produces misleading behavior at runtime.
findings.evidence: openapi_generator.py has try/except ImportError blocks that set RESTAPI = None and GraphQLAPI = None. Any code that later calls these as classes will get a TypeError: 'NoneType' is not callable — a confusing runtime error far from the actual missing import.
findings.suggestion: Replace the None fallbacks with explicit re-raise: `raise ImportError('openapi extra required: uv sync --extra openapi')` or use a lazy-import guard with a clear error message.

findings.summary: The 10 most-imported files in the codebase (the true critical paths) all have zero test coverage: plots/_base.py (20 importers), exceptions/base.py (12), components/_base.py (11), charts/plot_utils.py (8), ci_cd_automation/pipeline/models.py (6).
findings.evidence: desloppify scan reports 3,258 test files but the critical_untested list includes all of the highest-fan-in modules. Test effort is concentrated on leaf modules rather than the foundational abstractions that everything else builds on.
findings.suggestion: Add a test file specifically for each of the top-10 most-imported untested modules. Start with plots/_base.py and exceptions/base.py — they have the most dependents and will provide the highest regression safety.

findings.summary: event_logger.py repeats the same hasattr(event_type, 'value') guard pattern 5 times within ~100 lines without extraction into a helper.
findings.evidence: The pattern `event_type.value if hasattr(event_type, 'value') else str(event_type)` appears 5 times in event_logger.py. This is a hallmark of AI-generated code that copies snippets rather than extracting reusable helpers.
findings.suggestion: Extract a `_event_type_str(event_type) -> str` helper at module level and replace the 5 occurrences with a single call.

findings.summary: cerebrum/core/utils.py has its module docstring placed after the imports and logger initialization rather than at the top of the file.
findings.evidence: Python convention (PEP 257) requires module docstrings to be the first statement in a file. Placing them after imports is a common AI code generation artifact where the model generates imports first and then adds a docstring.
findings.suggestion: Move the module docstring to line 1 of the file, before any import statements.

findings.summary: Root-level maintenance scripts (fix_docstrings_v2.py, patch_basic_usage.py, patch_docs.py, test_gen.py, test_models.py) have 0 importers and no canonical home in a scripts/ or tools/ directory.
findings.evidence: 5 maintenance/utility scripts sit at the repository root with no importers. They are not part of the codomyrmex package, not in scripts/, and not documented as project tools. This pollutes the root namespace and makes them hard to discover.
findings.suggestion: Move to scripts/maintenance/ and add an entry to the root README or CONTRIBUTING.md that documents their purpose and usage.

findings.summary: Two parallel directories (scripts/docs/ and scripts/documentation/) serve overlapping documentation-management purposes with no clear ownership boundary.
findings.evidence: scripts/documentation/ has 21 files (flat overload). scripts/docs/ exists separately. data_visualization/ also has parallel engines/plot_utils.py and charts/plot_utils.py defining the same constants (DEFAULT_FIGURE_SIZE, DEFAULT_GRID_STYLE, DEFAULT_TITLE_FONTSIZE).
findings.suggestion: Merge scripts/docs/ and scripts/documentation/ into a single scripts/documentation/ directory with a clear sub-structure. Consolidate the duplicate plot_utils constants into a single source of truth.

findings.summary: bio_simulation/ and meme/ modules sit alongside production infrastructure without clear product-level justification, blurring the codebase's stated scope as a 'development toolbox for the PAI system'.
findings.evidence: The codebase CLAUDE.md describes codomyrmex as a development platform / PAI toolbox with 122 modules. bio_simulation and meme have no documented connection to that mission. They are listed in the module map but have no PAI integration docs.
findings.suggestion: Add a brief rationale to each module's README.md explaining how it serves the PAI toolbox mission, or move experimental/tangential modules to a separate top-level experiments/ package.

findings.summary: data_visualization/ has four separate _base.py files (plots/_base.py, components/_base.py, reports/_base.py, and one implicit) with overlapping abstract method signatures.
findings.evidence: Three distinct _base.py files in data_visualization/ each define abstract render/save/to_html patterns. The proliferation of parallel base hierarchies suggests the module grew organically without a unified visualization contract.
findings.suggestion: Define a single VisualizationBase protocol (PEP 544) or ABC in data_visualization/_base.py that all chart types, components, and report types implement, eliminating the three parallel hierarchies.

findings.summary: agents/cli/handlers.py passes (client_class, client_name, args) as a positional-typed tuple to _handle_agent_execute rather than a typed AgentRequest dataclass, making the CLI↔agent domain seam fragile.
findings.evidence: 15 public handler functions all call _handle_agent_execute(client_class, client_name, args) with three positional arguments. The seam between the CLI layer and the agent domain is a raw tuple rather than a typed contract. Any future parameter addition requires updating all 15 call sites.
findings.suggestion: Define an AgentInvocationRequest dataclass with client_class, client_name, and args fields. Have each of the 15 handler functions construct and pass a typed request object.

findings.summary: EventLogger subscribes to the event bus with a wildcard pattern during __init__, creating an implicit side effect at construction that makes testing and teardown difficult.
findings.evidence: EventLogger.__init__ calls self.event_bus.subscribe(['*'], self.log_event) unconditionally. This means constructing an EventLogger has the side effect of registering a global listener. Tests that create EventLogger instances will inadvertently accumulate active listeners.
findings.suggestion: Separate construction from activation: add a start() / stop() method pair. __init__ should only set up state; start() registers the subscription and stop() deregisters it. Use as a context manager for test isolation.

findings.summary: _setup_style() and _get_color_palette() in advanced_plotter.py use 10+ branch if-elif chains where a dict dispatch table would be shorter and more maintainable.
findings.evidence: _setup_style() has an if-elif chain with 10+ branches mapping style names to matplotlib.style.use() calls. _get_color_palette() has a similar chain. Both would be 3-4 lines with a dict: STYLE_MAP = {'default': 'seaborn', 'dark': 'dark_background', ...}; plt.style.use(STYLE_MAP.get(style, 'default'))
findings.suggestion: Replace both if-elif chains with module-level dict constants (STYLE_MAP, PALETTE_MAP) and a single dict.get() lookup with a default fallback.

findings.summary: security_analyzer.py has analysis methods with max_chain_depth 5 and chain_count 8, indicating deeply nested conditional logic that is hard to follow and test.
findings.evidence: desloppify scan flags security_analyzer.py with max_chain_depth=5, chain_count=8. Deep nesting in security analysis logic suggests the methods are doing too much: scanning, pattern matching, severity classification, and reporting in a single pass.
findings.suggestion: Break the deep nesting by extracting classification steps into dedicated methods: one for pattern matching, one for severity scoring, one for report generation. Early returns can flatten 3-4 nesting levels.

findings.summary: BasePlot in data_visualization mixes data attributes (title, width, height, data, options) with rendering concerns (to_html, save) and serialization utilities (to_dict, _fig_to_base64) in a single class.
findings.evidence: BasePlot has: data fields (title, data, options), render methods (render, to_html, show, save), and utility/serialization methods (to_dict, _fig_to_base64). These three concerns belong to different abstraction layers: the data model, the renderer, and the serializer.
findings.suggestion: Split BasePlot: keep data attributes in a PlotSpec dataclass, define a Renderer protocol with render/to_html/save, and move serialization helpers to a separate module-level utility. BasePlot can compose these via PlotRenderer(PlotSpec).

findings.summary: ConversationOrchestrator is a specialized infinite-conversation runner living in the generic 'agents' namespace rather than the orchestrator module where orchestration logic is centralized.
findings.evidence: agents/orchestrator.py contains ConversationOrchestrator, a specialized runner for infinite conversation loops. The orchestrator/ module already exists as the dedicated home for workflow/orchestration logic. Having an orchestrator class in the agents/ namespace creates design incoherence — users looking for orchestration functionality must check two places.
findings.suggestion: Move ConversationOrchestrator to orchestrator/conversation.py and import it in agents/ with a deprecation alias if backward compatibility is needed.
